_comment: Curated core of stable, high-value scholarships and internships for Morgan State CS students. This is the 'zero per-student API cost' layer from docs/plans/scholarships-v2.md option (b): the ~50-100 opportunities that barely change year to year. Live web search handles the long tail; this handles the awards worth applying to every year. RULES: never invent a deadline or a URL. Deadlines here are the annual application WINDOW ('opens fall', 'spring', 'rolling') because exact dates shift each cycle; the live link is the source of truth. Keep entries evergreen - if an award ends, remove it. eligibility_min_gpa is used for the same DegreeWorks filtering the live path uses.

scholarships.name: Morgan State Institutional Scholarships (ScholarshipUniverse)
scholarships.eligibility: Enrolled Morgan State students; one application matches you to many institutional and external awards.
scholarships.why: The single most important stop: one Morgan profile matches you to dozens of institutional and partner awards you'd otherwise never see.

scholarships.name: Morgan State Office of Financial Aid — Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: Morgan State students; FAFSA on file is usually required.
scholarships.why: Morgan's own need- and merit-based aid. Filing the FAFSA on time is what unlocks most of it.

scholarships.name: UNCF (United Negro College Fund) Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: African American students; many programs require a minimum 2.5–3.0 GPA and financial need.
scholarships.deadline: Rolling; individual programs have their own deadlines
scholarships.why: The largest source of scholarships for Black students; one UNCF profile opens many programs at once.

scholarships.name: Thurgood Marshall College Fund (TMCF) Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: Students at TMCF member HBCUs (Morgan State is a member); typically 3.0+ GPA.
scholarships.deadline: Varies by program; many open in spring
scholarships.why: Morgan is a TMCF member school, so you're directly eligible for its scholarships and its corporate recruiting pipeline.

scholarships.name: Jack Kent Cooke Foundation Undergraduate Transfer Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Community-college students transferring to a four-year school; high achievement and financial need.
scholarships.why: One of the most generous transfer awards in the country — worth it if you transferred into Morgan from a two-year school.

scholarships.name: Google Generation Scholarship (North America)
scholarships.eligibility: Students in computer science or a related field from historically marginalized groups.
scholarships.why: Built specifically for CS students from underrepresented groups — directly your field, and it comes with a retreat and community.

scholarships.name: Development Scholarships via Google (buildyourfuture)
scholarships.eligibility: CS and related-major students; several programs target HBCU students.
scholarships.why: Google runs multiple CS scholarships and dev programs; the hub lists which are open right now.

scholarships.name: Microsoft Tuition Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: Students pursuing CS, computer engineering, or a related STEM field; underrepresented groups encouraged.
scholarships.why: A tech-company tuition scholarship aimed squarely at CS majors from underrepresented backgrounds.

scholarships.name: SMART Scholarship-for-Service (Dept. of Defense)
scholarships.award: Full tuition + stipend + guaranteed DoD job
scholarships.eligibility: U.S. citizens in STEM fields; commit to work for the DoD after graduation.
scholarships.why: Pays full tuition plus a stipend and hands you a guaranteed job — the trade is a service commitment to the DoD.

scholarships.name: NSBE (National Society of Black Engineers) Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: NSBE members in engineering/CS fields.
scholarships.why: If you're a NSBE member (or join), it opens a whole catalog of engineering and CS awards plus its career fair.

scholarships.name: Congressional Black Caucus Foundation Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: African American students; some awards are tied to your congressional district.
scholarships.why: District-linked awards for Black students; being in a Maryland district can work in your favor.

scholarships.name: Bold.org Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: Open to a wide range of students; many no-essay and HBCU-specific awards.
scholarships.why: Low-effort, high-volume: many awards are quick to apply for, and some are HBCU- or CS-specific.

scholarships.name: ACM (Association for Computing Machinery) Student Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: ACM student members in computing fields.
scholarships.why: Being an ACM student member unlocks travel grants and research scholarships tied to the biggest CS conferences.

scholarships.name: AnitaB.org Grace Hopper Celebration Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Women and non-binary students in computing.
scholarships.why: Sends you to the largest gathering of women technologists in the world — the recruiting there is unmatched.

scholarships.name: Society of Women Engineers (SWE) Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: Women in ABET-accredited engineering and computing programs.
scholarships.why: One SWE application is scored against its entire scholarship pool, so a single form reaches many awards.

scholarships.name: Hispanic Scholarship Fund
scholarships.eligibility: Students of Hispanic heritage; 3.0 GPA and FAFSA required.
scholarships.why: The country's largest scholarship for Hispanic students, and it comes with career support, not just money.

scholarships.name: The Gates Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Minority high-achieving students with significant financial need (Pell-eligible).
scholarships.why: Covers everything tuition doesn't — one of the most complete rides in the country for high-need students.

scholarships.name: Coca-Cola Scholars Program
scholarships.eligibility: High school seniors (apply before college); leadership and service focus.
scholarships.why: A high-value, achievement-based award — worth flagging early for incoming students and anyone mentoring them.

scholarships.name: Maryland Higher Education Commission (MHEC) State Aid
scholarships.eligibility: Maryland residents; many awards require the FAFSA or MD State Financial Aid Application.
scholarships.why: State aid you're eligible for as a Maryland resident — filing by the March 1 priority date is what secures it.

scholarships.name: Cameron Impact Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: High-achieving high school seniors with leadership and community impact.
scholarships.why: A full-tuition merit award for standout students — flag it for incoming or transferring high-achievers.

scholarships.name: Davis-Putter Scholarship Fund
scholarships.eligibility: Students active in social-justice movements with financial need.
scholarships.why: Rewards community organizing and activism alongside academics — a fit if your service record is strong.

scholarships.name: Google Lime Scholarship (students with disabilities)
scholarships.eligibility: CS or related-field students who have a disability.
scholarships.why: A CS scholarship specifically for students with disabilities, plus access to Google's accessibility community.

internships.name: Google STEP Internship
internships.eligibility: First- and second-year undergraduates in CS or a related field; strong interest in software engineering.
internships.why: One of the few elite tech internships built for first- and second-years — apply early, before you think you're 'ready'.

internships.name: Microsoft Explore Internship
internships.eligibility: First- and second-year college students interested in software engineering and product.
internships.why: A rotational program for underclassmen — you try both engineering and PM before committing to a track.

internships.name: Meta University (MPK University) Internship
internships.eligibility: Rising juniors from underrepresented communities; coding experience but a non-traditional path is welcome.
internships.why: Explicitly built for students from underrepresented backgrounds — it values potential over a polished resume.

internships.name: Amazon Propel Program / SDE Internship
internships.eligibility: Undergraduates in CS; Propel supports students from underrepresented groups.
internships.why: Amazon hires a large SDE intern class and runs Propel to bring in underrepresented CS students.

internships.name: Apple Internships (SWE / Hardware)
internships.eligibility: Undergraduate and graduate students in CS/engineering.
internships.why: Broad intern program across many teams; apply early because roles fill on a rolling basis.

internships.name: NASA Internships (OSTEM)
internships.role: STEM research and engineering, including software
internships.eligibility: U.S. citizens; undergraduates in STEM with a 3.0+ GPA (varies by project).
internships.deadline: Three cycles a year; check portal
internships.why: Government research with real mission work; three cycles a year means more chances to land one.

internships.name: Capital One Technology Internship
internships.eligibility: Undergraduates in CS or related; Capital One recruits heavily at HBCUs.
internships.why: A strong HBCU recruiter close to Morgan geographically, with a large tech intern class.

internships.name: JPMorgan Chase Software Engineering Internship / Code for Good
internships.eligibility: Undergraduates in CS; Code for Good is an entry hackathon that feeds the internship.
internships.why: Code for Good is a low-barrier way in — do the hackathon, and it can turn into the summer internship.

internships.name: Morgan State Center for Career Development — Handshake
internships.why: Morgan-vetted postings on Handshake, including employers who specifically recruit here — start local.

internships.name: IBM Software / Data Science Internship
internships.eligibility: Undergraduates in CS/related; IBM recruits at HBCUs.
internships.why: Broad tech internships across software, data, and cloud, with an active HBCU recruiting presence.

internships.name: NSA Student Internship Programs
internships.eligibility: U.S. citizens; able to obtain a security clearance; strong GPA.
internships.why: Right in your backyard at Fort Meade — but the clearance takes months, so applying early is the whole game.

internships.name: Lockheed Martin Engineering / Software Internship
internships.eligibility: U.S. citizens in CS/engineering; many roles need clearance eligibility.
internships.why: Defense-sector software work with locations near Maryland; apply early since clearance roles move slowly.

internships.name: Northrop Grumman Software Engineering Internship
internships.eligibility: U.S. citizens in CS/engineering; clearance eligibility for many roles.
internships.why: A major Maryland-area defense employer with a large intern class and a strong HBCU pipeline.

internships.name: CodePath Summer Internship for Tech Excellence
internships.eligibility: CS students from underrepresented backgrounds; free CodePath courses feed it.
internships.why: Free CodePath courses double as a pipeline into partner internships — low barrier, high payoff for underrepresented students.

internships.name: Bank of America Global Technology Internship
internships.eligibility: Undergraduates in CS/related; active HBCU recruiting.
internships.why: Finance-tech roles with heavy HBCU recruiting; applications open very early, so watch for them in the summer.
